description: Address semantic review issues: activate ATR/variance ratio voters, use EA swing levels as fallback, fix breakeven MODIFY_SL retry logic, document acceptable limitations.
steps: 1. In main.py: Compute ATR from tick_collector._completed_bars using momentum.compute_atr (convert bars to DataFrame). Compute variance_ratio from completed bars. Pass current_atr, avg_atr, and variance_ratio to is_market_choppy() call.
2. In main.py: Use EA-provided ea_swing_high/ea_swing_low as fallback when tick_collector completed_bars has fewer than SWING_SL_LOOKBACK bars. Pass them to compute_swing_sl or use them directly.
3. In swing_levels.py: Add ea_swing_high/ea_swing_low parameters to compute_swing_sl so it can use EA values as fallback when Python bars are insufficient.
4. In trailing_stop.py: Fix breakeven MODIFY_SL - only set _be_moved=True AFTER successful write (move assignment after write_exit call, set back to False if exception occurs).
5. Add comments documenting acceptable limitations: single-minute race window (issue 4) and reversal detection cold start (issue 5).
acceptance_criteria: is_market_choppy() receives current_atr, avg_atr, and variance_ratio computed from completed bars
EA swing levels are used as fallback when Python bar buffer is insufficient
_be_moved is only set True after successful write_exit call
Issues 4 and 5 are documented with comments in the relevant code
